codexRework.trigger: Codex review flagged email #4 (index 3) and email #10 (index 9) as semantic duplicates: both 'part-time job causes section time conflict -> request to switch section -> ask procedure'.
codexRework.action: Kept email #4 (index 3, Psychology Friday discussion-section move, category 'course scheduling, absence, deadline, clarification'). Fully rewrote email #10 (index 9, category 'instructor, adviser, or teaching-assistant communication') into a different core problem with different communicative actions; matrix unchanged (7/7/6/6/6/6/6/6).

replaced.oldPrompt: You are a student enrolled in the afternoon section of a writing course, but a new part-time job means you can no longer attend in the afternoon. There is also a morning section of the same course. Write an email to the course instructor, Professor Choi. In your email, explain your scheduling problem, request permission to move to the morning section, and ask what you need to do to make the change official.
replaced.newPrompt: You are a second-year university student who hopes to specialize in environmental data analysis. Next term you can take only one of two electives that both seem related to this goal: "Geographic Information Systems" or "Statistics for Environmental Science." Because your academic adviser, Dr. Mensah, will approve your final course plan, you want her guidance before you decide. Write an email to Dr. Mensah. In your email, explain your specialization goal and why both electives appeal to you, ask which of the two would better support that goal and the reason for her recommendation, and ask whether choosing one of them now might limit other courses you may want to take in later terms.

replaced.category: housing, dining, transportation, or campus facilities
replaced.oldPrompt: You are a student who studies in the campus library most evenings. The lighting at several study desks on your floor has been flickering and some desks are completely dark, which makes reading uncomfortable. Write an email to the library facilities staff. In your email, describe the problem and where it is located, explain why it makes studying difficult, and ask when the lights are likely to be fixed.
replaced.newPrompt: You are a student who has recently started driving to campus each day because your class schedule changed. You would like to obtain a student parking permit so that you can park legally near your department building. Write an email to the campus Transportation and Parking Office. In your email, explain your situation and why you now need to drive to campus, ask about the eligibility requirements and the cost of a student parking permit, and ask whether a temporary permit is available to use while you are waiting on the permit waitlist.

repaired.reason: Duplicated the overused 'sick -> missed class -> how to catch up' trope already present at email index 1 (Chemistry/flu). Replaced with: request to submit an assignment as a recorded presentation instead of a written report (Prof Alvarez).

repaired.category: housing, dining, transportation, or campus facilities
repaired.reason: 'Booking system shows available but rejects' mechanic echoed the registration enrollment-error email (index 38) and the squatted study-room email (index 17). Replaced with: card-operated laundry machine took payment, frequently out of order (refund + repair).

'Right to repair / durability mandate' duplicated the technology-domain item at index 13 (Dr. Sorensen, device durability/e-waste). Replaced with a congestion-charge question (kept Dr. Cordova / Greta / Hassan).

'Car-free downtown zones' duplicated the environment-domain item at index 18 (Dr. Petrov, closing streets to cars). Replaced with short-term vacation-rental limits vs long-term housing (kept Dr. Quinteros / Pia / Rashid).

The two windows appear to have interpreted '60' differently (A: 60 new; B: total 60). Surfaced for reconciliation; Window B followed the explicit instruction.
